title: A Dual-Path Generative Framework for Zero-Day Fraud Detection in Banking Systems
optimized_headline: Discover a New Dual-Path Approach to Detect Zero-Day Banking Fraud

raw_body:
arXiv:2603.13237v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: High-frequency banking environments face a critical trade-off between low-latency fraud detection and the regulatory explainability demanded by GDPR. Traditional rule-based and discriminative models struggle with "zero-day" attacks due to extreme class imbalance and the lack of historical precedents. This paper proposes a Dual-Path Generative Framework that decouples real-time anomaly detection from offline adversarial training. The architecture employs a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) to establish a legitimate transaction manifold based on reconstruction error, ensuring <50ms inference latency. In parallel, an asynchronous Wasserstein GAN with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) synthesizes high-entropy fraudulent scenarios to stress-test the detection boundaries. Crucially, to address the non-differentiability of discrete banking data (e.g., Merchant Category Codes), we integrate a Gumbel-Softmax estimator. Furthermore, we introduce a trigger-based explainability mechanism where SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) is activated only for high-uncertainty transactions, reconciling the computational cost of XAI with real-time throughput requirements.
speedrun: A new paper presents a Dual-Path Generative Framework to improve fraud detection in high-frequency banking environments. This framework uses a Variational Autoencoder for real-time anomaly detection, achieving under 50 milliseconds of inference latency. It also employs a Wasserstein GAN to create synthetic fraud scenarios for testing. This approach is significant now as it balances the need for quick detection with regulatory requirements for explainability, addressing a critical challenge in modern banking.
why_it_matters: Banks can enhance fraud detection speeds while meeting GDPR regulations, benefiting both security teams and customers. This framework could lead to quicker responses to fraudulent activities.
The approach signals a shift towards more adaptive and explainable AI in finance, potentially setting new standards for fraud detection technology across the industry.

lenses.eli12: This new framework helps banks detect fraud faster and more accurately. Think of it like a security guard who can quickly identify suspicious behavior while also being able to explain their decisions. This matters because it could protect customers' money while keeping banks compliant with laws.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this framework meets user needs for quick fraud detection without sacrificing transparency. The low-latency detection could reduce costs associated with fraud losses, while the explainability feature enhances user trust. Implementing such technology could give companies a competitive edge in the market.
lenses.engineer: The Dual-Path Generative Framework combines a Variational Autoencoder for real-time detection and a Wasserstein GAN for stress-testing fraud scenarios. It maintains under 50ms latency while addressing data challenges with a Gumbel-Softmax estimator. This innovative architecture could significantly improve the robustness of fraud detection systems in banking.
